title: Abstract 1
story: I make a lot of figurative work — but I started with abstracts, fascinated with composition and the power of suggestion.

title: Abstract 2
story: Stripped back until there was nothing left but the sun, the buffalo, the tipis. And then the face and the tomahawk emerged.

title: Abstract 3
story: Vertical this time. Like a mesoamerican plinth filled with characters that tell stories older than time.

title: Abstract 4
story: It began as a series of shapes, and suddenly the face appeared. I thought about the patience of the hunter.

title: Abstract 5
story: Abstract feathers. Across the Americas, featherwork runs deep — ceremony, identity, status, prayer.

title: Afternoon Stroll
story: Not every piece is urgent. Sometimes it's just a walk in the afternoon light, moving through the land at a pace that lets you see it.

title: Ancestral Faces
story: For all the Indigenous faces that have ever existed in the Americas — and for the fact that their blood still runs in all of us.

title: Ancestral Woman
story: Mother, Grandmother, Healer, Warrior. She keeps coming back. I don't think she'll stop.

title: Beauty
story: I wanted to capture the beauty of the indigenous women in my life — Apache, Lakota, Ojibwe — around me now.

title: Bless This Lodge
story: A prayer for the home. For the structure that holds the family, that keeps the cold out, that becomes sacred just by being lived in.

title: Bride
story: About ceremony, and the things we do to mark the moments that matter — what we carry into the next part of life.

title: Brother Bear Fights Too Much
story: Bears fight. But Brother Bear has made it a lifestyle. Battered, still standing, still showing up. I know people like this.

title: Bullseye
story: Why not paint an indian with bow and arrow? We should celebrate the imagery, own it, take it back.

title: Dawn River Valley
story: Every river valley I've ever stood in at first light — that specific quiet, that specific colour, the feeling the world is about to start again.

title: Deer Woman
story: Deer Woman shows up across many Indigenous traditions. She's a shapeshifter. A warning. A destroyer of lecherous men.

title: Defender
story: For my grandmother — kind and happy, but made of iron. She would stop at nothing to defend her family and land.

title: Defending a Way of Life
story: A Mesoamerican warrior, war club raised. His face is grim — this is not a man who takes what he's doing lightly.

title: Destroy ICE
story: This is not subtle and it's not meant to be. ICE is a weapon used against Indigenous and brown people.

title: Embera Woman
story: My mother's people are Ngäbe-Buglé from Panama. The Emberá are their neighbors. A portrait of a woman bathing at the river.

title: Fight Back
story: What if the native kids swept into residential schools could fight back? What if the hatchet could be turned against oppressors?

title: Fractured Native
story: Identity fragmented by colonial pressure, by blood quantum laws, by the constant demand to prove you're Native enough.

title: Fuck ICE
story: ICE terrorizes Indigenous and immigrant communities. It separates families. It operates with near-total impunity.

title: Fury
story: A rage that doesn't explode — it concentrates. The fierceness it took to not back down against the Spanish conquerors.

title: Futurism: Always Carry a Tomahawk
story: Tradition and technology were never opposites. Also a wink at Da Vinci — this is my Mona Lisa.

title: Futurism: Crash Landing
story: Not every mission goes clean. Indigenous resilience isn't about perfect landings — it's about getting back up.

title: Futurism: Day of Reckoning
story: The sense that something long overdue is finally arriving. Indigenous futures aren't just possible, they're inevitable.

title: Futurism: Defiant
story: Out of options, cornered by the guns and drones of a futuristic government, this indigenous woman takes a defiant stance.

title: Futurism: Defiant Forever
story: Not defiant for a moment or a movement — forever. Generations of resistance, encoded in the stance, in the refusal to disappear.

title: Futurism: Flyby
story: Speed, precision, purpose. All happening at speeds you can barely measure.

title: Futurism: Future So Bright
story: Refusing the narrative that Indigenous people are a past-tense people. We are flying spaceships and wearing excellent sunglasses.

title: Futurism: In Command
story: Indigenous people in charge of things — not as fantasy, but as a correction of a historical wrong. The first in the Futurism series.

title: Futurism: Indiginaut
story: Indigenous in space — not as metaphor, but as fact. The Indiginaut carries the land with them into the cosmos.

title: Futurism: Insight
story: The insight is that we were always here, always watching, always knowing. The future doesn't surprise us.

title: Futurism: Join the Fleet
story: Recruitment poster energy, except the fleet is ours. Indigenous sovereignty in the future looks like a navy.

title: Futurism: Keep Them Flying
story: A tribute to the ones who keep going and keep everything else going — machines, yes, but also ceremony, language, memory.

title: Futurism: Liftoff
story: Liftoff isn't just mechanical — it's the decision to go, to reach, to claim something that was always yours.

title: Futurism: Mesoamerican
story: Mesoamerican civilizations built cities that rivalled anything in Europe. What does that sophistication look like projected forward?

title: Futurism: Our Land
story: Not a request. Not a protest sign. A statement of fact that predates every map ever drawn by a government that didn't ask.

title: Futurism: Sentinel
story: The sentinel watches. Has always watched. There's a patience in guardianship that spans generations.

title: Futurism: Silicon Tattoos
story: Technology and tradition aren't opposites. Silicon tattoos are just another form of adornment, another way of declaring who you are.

title: Futurism: Spacewalk
story: Out here, there's no treaty line, no reservation border, no jurisdiction. The spacewalk is freedom.

title: Futurism: The Negotiator
story: The best negotiations are won by someone who knows the terrain better than anyone across the table. That's this figure.

title: Futurism: Warrior Society
story: The warrior societies didn't dissolve. They evolved. Tradition of protection and purpose, updated for any century.

title: Glorious Morning
story: Some mornings are just objectively glorious. The sun rising over a group of tipis, everything bathed in glorious pastels.

title: Hunter
story: Stillness before the shot. The hunter isn't moving — everything inside is absolutely focused, and the land holds its breath.

title: March of the Buffalo
story: There were 30–60 million bison. Then fewer than a thousand. The slaughter was deliberate. The herds are coming back.

title: MMIW
story: Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women — not a statistic. It is a mother, a sister, a daughter, a friend.

title: Moonlight Fishing
story: Thinking about woodland ancestors in birchbark canoes. Not the heroic war party — the quieter moment. Fishing at night.

title: Morning Prayers
story: Starting the day with intention changes the day. The moment before you've been anywhere or done anything.

title: Mother and Child
story: About transmission — the way culture, language, knowledge, and survival instinct get passed from body to body.

title: Native Festival
story: Growing up in Central America there were lots of festivals. In almost all of them were super bright spots of indigeneity.

title: Peaceful, Not Harmless
story: We will not escalate. But peaceful is not the same as harmless, and patience is not the same as passivity.

title: Raven with Petroglyphs and Weapons
story: Ravens remember everything. Petroglyphs are memory made permanent. The raven ascends, but never forgets his roots.

title: Resist
story: One word. The history of Indigenous people is a history of resistance — the word has lived in our bones longer than the movement borrowed it.

title: Sacred Lodge
story: The lodge is not just a structure. It's a container for ceremony, for prayer, for the kind of conversation you don't have anywhere else.

title: Sun-Kissed Warrior
story: The warrior tradition is not primarily about war. It's about protection. This figure is standing in the sun, unbothered. Ready.

title: Sun Lodge
story: The sun comes in through the smoke hole and hits the floor of the lodge and everything stops. Light as ceremony.

title: Sun Over Tipis
story: The tipi is engineered to perfection. This piece is about having a relationship with your shelter rather than just occupying it.

title: There Are Two Wolves Inside You
story: The idea that we are in constant negotiation with our worst and best selves. The story is true even if the attribution is messy.

title: Thirteen Moon Turtle
story: Thirteen moons, thirteen plates on the turtle's back. The lunar calendar lives in the animal. The turtle carries time and memory.

title: Three Buffalo
story: The Flatirons at night with the Milky Way overhead and three buffalo. The animal, the mountain, the cosmos — all at once.

title: Three Crows in Winter
story: Winter doesn't silence crows. They're louder in the cold, more visible against the snow. Can you imagine the stories they're telling?

title: Three Sachems
story: For the chiefs I've known throughout the Americas — wise and funny and full of stories. Leaders by trust, not title.

title: Thunderbird Lodge
story: The Thunderbird presides — a lodge held under its protection, who brings rain and lightning and the kind of power that clears the air.

title: Thunderbird Petroglyph
story: The Thunderbird has been carved into rock faces across the continent for thousands of years. I'm the latest in that long chain.

title: Uncle
story: Made in honor of all the uncles I've seen at powwow — keeping up the traditions, carrying the knowledge forward.

title: Undefeated
story: Not unscathed. Undefeated. One is about being untouched, the other is about what you do with the damage.

title: Walk in the Woods
story: The woods are not quiet for those who know how to listen. Every step has been walked by someone before you.

title: War Club and Tomahawk
story: Two weapons, two traditions, one purpose: protection. A statement of continuity across centuries.

title: We Are All Connected
story: The ecological reality is that everything is connected. Indigenous peoples have been saying this for as long as there have been Indigenous peoples.

title: White Buffalo
story: The birth of a white buffalo calf is a sacred sign in Lakota tradition — a prophecy being fulfilled, a call to unity and healing.

title: Wolf Singing
story: Not howling — singing. Singing implies intention. Community. A voice raised toward something. This wolf is mid-song.

title: Abstract 6
story: The sixth in the series. The shapes keep evolving — this time pulling from memory, not intention. Something between a glyph and a dream.

title: Ancestors Point the Way
story: They came before us and they're still here — in the land, in the blood, in the way we move through the world. This is that sense of direction that comes from deep time.

title: Buffalo Dream
story: A vision. The buffalo that ran across this continent in their millions are not entirely gone — they persist in dreams and ceremony and art and the slow memory of the land.

title: Canyon Sunrise
story: First light hitting red rock. Canyon country has been home to people for thousands of years. This is dawn from the rim — that moment when the world remembers it's alive.

title: Curious Cubs
story: Two young bears discovering the world. Everything is still new to them. I wanted to catch that moment of pure curiosity — no fear yet, just wonder.

title: Floating
story: Between earth and sky. The feeling when you're up high enough that the weight of everything releases. Inspired by ceremony — the way the right moment can make you feel untethered from gravity.

title: Futurism: On Patrol
story: Indigenous futurism means we're not just survivors — we're guardians. On patrol is about vigilance, about the knowledge that protection of land and people is sacred work that never ends.

title: Futurism: Ready to Go
story: The moment before departure. In the future I imagine, readiness looks like this — equipped with tradition, carrying the old knowledge, aimed at tomorrow.

title: Futurism: Trigger Discipline
story: Power without recklessness. In my futurism, the warriors have discipline — they know the difference between strength and cruelty. Trigger discipline is more than technique; it's a philosophy of restraint.

title: Futurism: We Are Star Stuff
story: Indigenous peoples have always known what Carl Sagan later named — we come from the stars and we will return to them. This is the cosmic dimension of native identity, rendered in light.

title: Gathering of Clans
story: Nations coming together. This is what sovereignty looks like from the inside — not isolation but connection, ceremony, the deliberate act of gathering to remember who we are and what we carry.

title: Joy
story: We are still here. And sometimes, we are joyful. This is not about forgetting — it's about refusing to let grief be the whole story. Joy is resistance. Joy is survival made visible.

title: Medicine Wheel Crow
story: The medicine wheel maps the cosmos — four directions, four seasons, four stages of life. The crow carries that knowledge, watches the wheel turn, knows exactly what it means.

title: Petroglyph Hunter
story: Cut into rock thousands of years ago, the hunter persists. These figures are not primitive — they are precise communications across deep time. Every stroke was intentional. Every mark is still speaking.

title: Stand Your Ground
story: The phrase gets weaponized by the wrong people. This is what it looks like when it means something true — when standing your ground means protecting your people, your land, your way of life.

title: The Nesting Place
story: Home is wherever the young are kept safe. This is a study in nesting — the precision and care that animals bring to making a place where new life can begin and be protected.

title: Three Sister Wolves
story: Three wolves, three sisters. The wolf pack is organized around relationship — the particular intimacy of sisters, which in wolf society is the foundation of everything. Pack is family. Family is survival.

title: Timeless Vigil
story: Watching without end. The old ones kept vigil — over the young, over the sacred places, over the knowledge that keeps us whole. This is that practice, rendered in light and color across time.

title: Resist: Yesterday, Today, Tomorrow
story: Resistance isn't a moment — it's a continuum. Our ancestors resisted. We resist now. Those who come after us will resist too. This is what that looks like when you collapse the timeline: the same fire, carried forward through every generation.
